sprint.title: Studio Orientation v1: Portrait ↔ Landscape Toggle (Page Presets + Safe Area + Export)
sprint.goal: Add an Orientation toggle in Studio to support horizontal (landscape) books. Switching orientation updates page presets, safe/bleed boxes, and template layout mapping, and is respected by PDF export.
definitionOfDone: Studio has an Orientation toggle: Portrait / Landscape in footer
Orientation is stored per storybook (and optionally per document preset)
Changing orientation updates page width/height (preset swap) and recomputes safe/bleed boxes
All pages render correctly in both orientations (continuous + single-page modes remain functional)
Populate-to-Studio uses orientation-aware templates (or remaps layouts safely)
Export respects orientation and produces correct PDF page sizes
UI clearly warns if switching orientation may require layout adjustments
No content ends up outside safe area by default after orientation change (clamp pass runs)

stackAssumptions.export: PDF renderer consumes page preset dimensions

backlog.title: Define orientation presets and print-safe geometry (portrait/landscape variants)

notes: Landscape uses width > height; safe/bleed recalculated with new dimensions.
constraints: Preset must be compatible with your eventual print partners (Blurb/Lulu etc.)

acceptanceTests: Preset objects exist and compute safeBox/bleedBox deterministically

backlog.title: Persist orientation per storybook/document (Convex schema + migration default)

storage: default to portrait for existing books
constraints: Changing orientation updates preset id; keep stable doc ids

acceptanceTests: Orientation can be saved and restored on refresh

backlog.title: Studio UI: Orientation toggle (Portrait/Landscape) + confirmation modal

ui: Toggle in Studio top bar or settings drawer
Modal warning: switching orientation may move/rescale content

acceptanceTests: Toggle updates the editor view immediately after confirmation

backlog.title: Orientation switch engine: remap page dimensions and run clamp/fit pass

behavior: Swap page width/height based on preset
Run clamp pass for nodes: keep text inside safeBox
For images/frames: preserve aspect via crop; clamp to safe unless full-bleed layout
constraints: Be deterministic and reversible (switch back works)

acceptanceTests: After switching, nodes are still visible and inside safe area (text)

backlog.title: Template/layout mapping: choose portrait vs landscape layouts per chapter/page

templateChange: Add optional layout variants per page: layoutIdPortrait, layoutIdLandscape
If landscape variant missing, fallback to portrait layout with auto-fit
constraints: No template explosion: only maintain variants for key pages first
Keep slot ids stable across variants when possible

acceptanceTests: Landscape uses landscape layouts when available
Fallback path works when variant missing

backlog.title: Export respects orientation (PDF page sizes + safe/bleed boxes)

behavior: PDF renderer uses storybook preset orientation for page size
Optional debug overlay for safe/bleed (dev only)
constraints: No distortion; content renders in the rotated geometry

acceptanceTests: Landscape export pages are wider than tall

backlog.title: QA + tests (switching, layout fallback, export)

manualChecklist: Switch portrait -> landscape -> portrait and confirm content survives
Check text stays inside safe area after switch
Populate + open studio in landscape preset
Export landscape PDF and verify page dimensions

acceptanceTests: At least 3 automated tests added and passing
No regressions to Studio pages scroll modes

notes: Landscape books are commonly used for photo books and panoramic content; providers like Blurb explicitly offer standard landscape formats. :contentReference[oaicite:3]{index=3}
Changing orientation requires recomputing safe/bleed/margins; print guidance emphasizes margins and bleed considerations. :contentReference[oaicite:4]{index=4}
